channel.title: Newsletter | FORRT - Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training
channel.copyright: © 2026 - FORRT > Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training

item.title: FORRT Newsletter — May 2026
item.description:
Announces the Replication Hub Showcase (1 July 2026, online) and a ‘CRediTing in BTS’ hackathon at SIPS 2026 in Washington, DC, plus a call for validators for FLoRA, FORRT’s replication database of over 2,000 study pairs. Applications are also open for the LMU Open Science Summer School 2026 (deadline 6 July).

Read the newsletter

item.title: FORRT Newsletter — April 2026
item.description:
Contains a call for contributors to the FLoRA replication library, alongside the new interactive Re-Search Terms app. It also invites responses to the Big Team Science Experience and Tools & Infrastructure surveys (deadline 18 May).

Read the newsletter

item.title: FORRT Newsletter — March 2026
item.description:
Shares the launch of Round 3 of the Predicting Replicability Challenge (22 April). It also highlights recordings from Love Replications Week and the Zotero Replication Checker, now available in five languages.

Read the newsletter

item.title: FORRT Newsletter — February 2026
item.description:
Previews Love Replications Week (2–6 March), a Team Credit Hackathon (9 March), and the in-person Making Replications Count Hackathon (4–6 May). It introduces new tools — ARIADNE, the FReD Explorer App, and the FLoRA Annotator — with the FReD database now holding over 1,000 replication findings.

Read the newsletter

item.title: FORRT Newsletter — January 2026
item.description:
Marks the launch of the Replication Research (R²) journal with its inaugural editorial, and introduces the FORWARD (FORRT for World Development) initiative aligned with UNESCO’s International Decade of Sciences for Sustainable Development. It also flags upcoming events including Love Replications Week and the Making Replications Count Hackathon.

Read the newsletter
